questions.question: Explain the meaning of political institutions.
questions.answer: Political institutions are organizations that help govern a country and make decisions according to rules and procedures.

questions.question: Explain why institutions are important in democratic governance.
questions.answer: Institutions ensure decisions are taken through proper procedures and prevent misuse of power.

questions.question: Explain the policy decision process in India.
questions.answer: Major decisions are taken by the Cabinet, discussed in Parliament and implemented by government departments.

questions.question: Explain the Mandal Commission and its purpose.
questions.answer: The Mandal Commission identified socially and educationally backward classes and recommended reservation in government jobs.

questions.question: Explain the main functions of Parliament.
questions.answer: Parliament makes laws, controls government finances and discusses national issues.

questions.question: Explain the composition of the Indian Parliament.
questions.answer: The Indian Parliament consists of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: Explain the role of Lok Sabha.
questions.answer: Lok Sabha represents the people and controls the government through debates and motions.

questions.question: Explain the role of Rajya Sabha.
questions.answer: Rajya Sabha represents the states and reviews laws passed by the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Explain the difference between Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
questions.answer: Lok Sabha members are directly elected while Rajya Sabha members are indirectly elected and represent states.

questions.question: Explain the powers of Parliament over government finances.
questions.answer: Parliament approves the budget and controls public expenditure.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of executive.
questions.answer: Executive refers to the branch of government responsible for implementing laws and policies.

questions.question: Explain the difference between political and permanent executive.
questions.answer: Political executives are elected leaders while permanent executives are civil servants.

questions.question: Explain the role of civil servants.
questions.answer: Civil servants assist ministers in implementing policies and administration.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Prime Minister.
questions.answer: The Prime Minister leads the Cabinet and coordinates government activities.

questions.question: Explain the functions of the Council of Ministers.
questions.answer: The Council of Ministers assists the Prime Minister in policy making and governance.

questions.question: Explain the role of the President.
questions.answer: The President is the head of the state and acts according to the advice of the Council of Ministers.

questions.question: Explain the election process of the President.
questions.answer: The President is elected by elected MPs and MLAs.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of judiciary.
questions.answer: Judiciary refers to the system of courts responsible for interpreting laws and resolving disputes.

questions.question: Explain the structure of the Indian judiciary.
questions.answer: The judiciary consists of Supreme Court, High Courts and lower courts.

questions.question: Explain judicial review.
questions.answer: Judicial review allows courts to examine laws and declare them unconstitutional.

questions.question: Explain Public Interest Litigation.
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to approach courts for issues affecting public interest.

questions.question: Explain judicial independence.
questions.answer: Judicial independence ensures fair and unbiased judgments.

questions.question: Explain the concept of checks and balances.
questions.answer: Checks and balances prevent concentration of power among institutions.

questions.question: Explain why parliamentary debates are important.
questions.answer: Debates help representatives discuss policies and represent public concerns.

questions.question: Explain how institutions strengthen democracy.
questions.answer: Institutions ensure fair decision making, accountability and power distribution.
